document.title: Collage Journal: Layers of Noise
document.description: Mixing risograph textures, ink bleed, and found typography.

body.raw:
I keep a box of scraps from every print run—misaligned layers, overexposed scans, the mistakes. They land in the collage journal when the timing feels right.

## Studio ritual

- Stack translucent papers to test how light leaks through.
- Annotate every layer with china marker arrows and timestamps.
- Photograph the spreads in the same window light for continuity.

> Imperfection is proof the work passed through real hands.

document.title: Ink Study: Learning the Weight of Water
document.description: Experimenting with blurred edges, reclaimed paper, and the rhythm of washes.

body.raw:
## Palette

- Walnut ink
- Indigo powder
- Rice paper rescued from the recycling stack

The page buckles when it dries and that is half the charm. I layer washes, lift pigment with salt, then leave the mistakes visible so the piece keeps breathing.

> Each bloom is a timestamp of the brush pausing for breath.

document.title: Neighborhood Assemblies at Sunrise
document.description: Organizing civic listening sessions before the rush of the day.

body.raw:
We invite neighbors upstairs while the city is still yawning. The open air keeps the conversation soft, and everyone brings a note from the block they represent.

## Ground rules we revisit

1. Listen in full sentences before proposing fixes.
2. Capture ideas in the open so credit stays visible.
3. Treat disagreement as a design problem, not a dead end.

*“Policy is just community care written down.”*

document.title: Tracing the Shape of Code
document.description: Sketching an architecture-first approach to building personal sites.

body.raw:
# Tracing the Shape of Code

Designing a personal site starts with the constraints. For this build, the north star is a static export that still feels alive.

> Static-first does not mean lifeless—it means intentional about what truly needs to run on the client.

## Notes from the planning sketch

- Contentlayer owns the data model so we keep MDX portable.
- Light/dark palettes stay in CSS custom properties.
- Keep every fancy animation behind `prefers-reduced-motion` checks.[^motion]

```tsx
export function loadPosts() {
  return sortByDate(allPosts);
}
```

[^motion]: We owe accessibility more than a quick toggle.
